title: Connection Factory Management
description: Main connection provisioning, new connection creation, and connection lifecycle management for JDBC connection factories

steps: STEP: call delegate.newConnection() immediately → assign result to mainConnection field
END: factory is ready with a pre-created mainConnection
STEP: return pre-created mainConnection (no new allocation)
END: caller receives singleton main JdbcConnection
STEP: delegate creates and returns a fresh T instance
END: caller receives a new (non-main) JdbcConnection
DECISION: databaseName provided? NO → default databaseName = 'mysql'
STEP: call forTestDatabase(databaseName) with resolved name
STEP: ServiceLoader.load(TestConnectionProvider.class) — scan classpath for provider implementations
BRANCH NO: orElseThrow() — throw NoSuchElementException, END (error)
START: concrete provider calls super(config, factory) from BinlogTestConnection
STEP: addDefaultSettings(config) — apply default JDBC settings

branching_conditions: newConnection() vs mainConnection(): mainConnection() returns cached instance; newConnection() always delegates to factory
forTestDatabase() overload: zero-arg variant defaults databaseName='mysql', then calls one-arg variant
ServiceLoader resolution: findFirst().orElseThrow() — exactly one provider required on classpath; absent provider throws NoSuchElementException
Provider dispatch: runtime SPI determines whether MySql or MariaDb provider is used
important_notes: Connection provisioning context: parallel snapshotting — one dedicated 'main' connection plus on-demand new connections
DefaultMainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory eagerly creates mainConnection in constructor (not lazy)
ConnectionFactory<T> is @FunctionalInterface — can be supplied as a lambda
TestConnectionService is a stateless utility class (no fields, all static methods)
SPI registration is via META-INF/services files per connector module (mysql, mariadb)
BinlogTestConnection is abstract; concrete types provided by connector-specific subclasses
